articles.title: EPA approves sale of higher ethanol fuel to try to lower gas prices
articles.description:
The sale of E15 is typically discontinued in the summer because it can contribute to harmful air pollution.

The post EPA approves sale of higher ethanol fuel to try to lower gas prices appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: EPA approves E15 fuel sale to help reduce gas prices for Boston residents.
E15, a higher ethanol blend, typically banned in summer due to pollution concerns.
Local drivers may benefit from lower fuel costs amid rising gas prices.

articles.title: In the midst of the MVP race, Jaylen Brown explains his stance on the NBA’s 65-game rule
articles.description:
"You can’t have dudes playing 45, 50 games and winning first-team All-NBA. You basically came to work half the time and got rewarded for it."

The post In the midst of the MVP race, Jaylen Brown explains his stance on the NBA’s 65-game rule appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jaylen Brown criticizes NBA's 65-game rule for MVP eligibility during the ongoing MVP race.
Brown argues players shouldn't earn All-NBA honors after playing only 45 to 50 games.
His comments may influence future discussions on player participation and awards criteria in Boston.

articles.title: Jury finds Instagram and YouTube liable in landmark social media addiction trial
articles.description:
After more than 40 hours of deliberation across nine days, California jurors decided Meta and YouTube were negligent in the design or operation of their platforms.

The post Jury finds Instagram and YouTube liable in landmark social media addiction trial appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jury finds Meta and YouTube negligent in social media design after nine days of deliberation.
Trial highlights growing concerns over social media addiction affecting Boston youth.
Potential for increased regulation on social media platforms impacting local users.

articles.title: ‘It felt like a gut punch’: Pride flags removed from BU offices ignite free speech, inclusion debate
articles.description:
Faculty and students are challenging BU’s removal of Pride flags, arguing the enforcement of a “content-neutral” signage policy undermines free expression.

summary: Boston University removed Pride flags from offices, sparking debate on free speech policies.
Faculty and students argue the removal contradicts inclusion efforts at BU.
The incident highlights tensions around expression and representation within the university community.

articles.title: A JetBlue flight reportedly hit a coyote during takeoff at T.F. Green Airport
articles.description:
The captain told passengers, "This is the flight crew. If anybody heard that thud, we hit a coyote," according to a report.

The post A JetBlue flight reportedly hit a coyote during takeoff at T.F. Green Airport appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: JetBlue flight hit a coyote during takeoff at T.F. Green Airport.
Captain confirmed incident to passengers, noting a loud thud was heard.
Local wildlife safety concerns may arise for nearby residents and travelers.
